title: Purchasable upgrade
description: A ship upgrade, piece of personal equipment, or gadget sold at stations with the `shipyard` service. Buying one deducts credits, records the id in the save's player.upgrades, and sets the player flag `upgrade_<id>` so trigger-DSL conditions and dialogue can see it. `effects` is a flat bag of named numeric/boolean modifiers; engine systems read the ones they understand and ignore the rest — a mod can invent effects its own scripts consume. Nothing promises an upgrade is useful.

category.description: Shop shelf: ship systems, personal/cybernetic equipment, or widgets that may or may not help.

effects.description: Named modifiers, aggregated across owned upgrades. Known to the engine: hull_bonus (adds to hull integrity, 0..1), damage_bonus (extra weapon damage per hit, integer), detection_mult (multiplies hostile patrol detection ranges, <1 = stealthier; multiplicative across upgrades), speed_mult (multiplies top speed), timer_bonus_seconds (added to mission stage failure timers when the stage starts). Unknown keys are preserved for mod scripts.

flavor.description: Merchant's pitch, in the seller's voice.

extra.description: Free-form escape hatch for mod-specific data. Never read by the engine.
